auth.signInDesc: Enter your credentials to access NetPulse
auth.registerSuccess: Account created successfully! Please sign in.
auth.loginFailed: Login failed. Please check your credentials and try again.
auth.createAccountDesc: Register for a new NetPulse account
auth.forbidden: You don't have permission to access this page.
auth.tokenRevoked: Your session has been revoked. Please sign in again.
auth.loginRateLimited: Too many login attempts. Please try again later.
auth.registerRateLimited: Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.
auth.registerRemoved: The registration API has been removed by the backend. Sign in with an administrator account.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.healthDescription: Aggregate current monitoring status by Target, Agent, and protocol.
dashboard.healthLoadFailed: Failed to load network health data
dashboard.healthLoadFailedDesc: Unable to read the monitoring task list. Dashboard conclusions are paused.
dashboard.attentionQueueDesc: No-data, failed, and MTR tasks first.
dashboard.protocolDistributionDesc: Used to confirm whether Targets have complete ICMP/TCP/MTR/IPERF3 detection coverage.
dashboard.selectTargetForEvidence: Select a Target to view evidence.
dashboard.createTaskHint: Create a task to start monitoring

agents.title: Agents
agents.dialogDesc: Register a new monitoring agent with location tags.
agents.createFailed: Failed to create agent. Please try again.
agents.createdDesc: Save this access key now. It will not be shown again.
agents.installCommandHint: Run this command on the target machine to install and start the agent.
agents.disableAgentConfirm: Are you sure you want to disable {{name}}? This agent will stop receiving tasks.
agents.enableAgentConfirm: Are you sure you want to enable {{name}}? This agent will start receiving tasks again.
agents.noTasksAssigned: No tasks assigned to this agent.
agents.releasesDesc: Manage agent binary releases across platforms.
agents.uploadReleaseDesc: Upload a new agent binary release.
agents.deleteReleaseConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete v{{version}} for {{platform}}?

agentAdmin.title: Agent Management
agentAdmin.description: Manage agents, status, and configuration sync information through /api/v1/agents/*.
agentAdmin.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, city, or carrier
agentAdmin.editDialogDesc: After Agent management information changes, the backend republishes that Agent task snapshot.
agentAdmin.createDialogDesc: Creation returns a one-time auth_token. Save it to the Agent configuration immediately.
agentAdmin.zipPlaceholder: Postal code, use UNKNOWN when unknown
agentAdmin.createdDialogDesc: auth_token is returned only once in the creation response.

adminInputs.tagPlaceholder: Enter a tag and search, Enter to add

settings.title: System Settings
settings.description: Manage backend runtime configuration through the API. Worker parameter changes are read by related workers on the next loop.
settings.saveFailed: Save failed. Check parameter ranges and backend logs.
settings.victoriametricsDesc: Write and query addresses for ICMP/TCP time-series metrics. Workers use the write address; API charts use the query address.
settings.resultWorkerDesc: Controls NATS result worker fetch batch size, wait time, and in-process concurrency.
settings.resultFetchBatchSizeDesc: Number of result messages fetched from NATS per pull.
settings.resultFetchTimeoutDesc: Maximum seconds to wait for NATS messages.
settings.processingConcurrencyDesc: Maximum concurrent message handlers in one worker process.
settings.agentStateWorkerDesc: Worker parameters for Agent heartbeat and task configuration ACK handling.
settings.agentStateFetchBatchSizeDesc: Number of heartbeat or ACK messages fetched per pull.
settings.agentStateFetchTimeoutDesc: Maximum seconds to wait for Agent status messages.
settings.agentOfflineAfterDesc: Mark Agent offline after this many seconds without heartbeat.
settings.agentStatusWorkerDesc: Periodically sweeps Agent status and can use PostgreSQL advisory lock for singleton execution.
settings.agentStatusSweepIntervalDesc: Agent status sweep interval in seconds.
settings.agentStatusSingletonDesc: When enabled, only the worker instance that obtains the advisory lock performs the sweep.
settings.asnEnrichmentWorkerDesc: Controls MTR hop ASN enrichment scheduling frequency, claim batch size, and stale claim recycling.
settings.asnSweepIntervalDesc: ASN enrichment worker sweep interval in seconds.
settings.asnBatchSizeDesc: Number of MTR results claimed per batch.
settings.asnClaimStaleDesc: Claims older than this can be recycled by another worker.
settings.asnSingletonDesc: When enabled, ASN enrichment sweep is forced to singleton execution.
settings.resultIngestionEventsDesc: Result ingestion events help diagnose NATS consumption and result-write paths.
settings.cloudflareR2Desc: Cloudflare R2 configuration for Agent Artifact upload, download, and presigned URLs.
settings.r2EndpointDesc: Cloudflare R2 S3 API endpoint URL.
settings.r2SecretDesc: Plaintext is not returned. Leave empty when saving to keep the current secret.
settings.r2BucketDesc: R2 Bucket used to store Agent Artifacts.
settings.publicBaseUrlDesc: Optional. When empty, the backend uses presigned S3 URLs.
settings.downloadUrlTtlDesc: Presigned download URL lifetime in seconds.
settings.uploadMaxBytesDesc: Maximum Agent Artifact upload size in bytes.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.createFailed: Failed to create task. Please try again.
tasks.noAgentsAssigned: No agents assigned to this task.
tasks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
tasks.manageAgentsDesc: Toggle agents assigned to this task.
tasks.managementDesc: Manage tasks through /api/v1/tasks/* and use /api/v1/relations/quick-associate to create Target and Agent relationships in bulk.
tasks.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, Target, or Agent
tasks.quickAssociateSuccess: Quick association completed, created or reused {{count}} tasks
tasks.createDialogDesc: A Task must bind one Target and one Agent, and the selected protocol must be supported by the Target.
tasks.nameOptionalDesc: Optional. When empty, the backend generates a default name from Agent, Target, protocol, and IP family.
tasks.nameEditDesc: Optional. When saved empty, the backend regenerates a default name from the current binding and protocol.
tasks.targetDesc: Select the target to probe. The protocol list follows the Target supported_protocols setting.
tasks.agentDesc: Select the node that executes the task. The task is delivered to this Agent scheduler.
tasks.protocolDesc: Supports ICMP, TCP, MTR, and IPERF3. IPERF3 runs once per day and stores results in PostgreSQL.
tasks.ipFamilyDesc: Choose whether the Agent probes over IPv4 or IPv6. Target and Agent must have matching connectivity.
tasks.ipFamilyEditDesc: Choose whether the Agent probes over IPv4 or IPv6.
tasks.quickDialogDesc: Select one Target and one Agent. The backend creates the default tasks for that relationship.
tasks.quickTargetDesc: Select the target to associate. The backend creates default tasks based on supported protocols.
tasks.quickAgentDesc: Select the Agent that executes the default tasks. Association triggers task snapshot sync.
tasks.editDialogDesc: Modify task name, binding, IP family, scheduling fields, and protocol-specific probe_config. Task type is fixed.
tasks.targetUnsupported: The current Target does not declare support for {{protocol}}.
tasks.editProtocolDesc: Task type is fixed after creation. IPERF3 runs once per day.
tasks.deleteDialogDesc: Soft delete {{name}}? The backend will publish a new Agent task snapshot after deletion.
tasks.protocolIperf3Desc: IPERF3 runs once per day and includes upload and download actions. Results are stored in PostgreSQL.
tasks.protocolTcpDesc: TCP connect probes run at minute-level intervals to observe port reachability, connection latency, jitter, and loss.
tasks.protocolMtrDesc: MTR stores per-hop packet_loss_pct, avg_ms, best_ms, and worst_ms to locate path quality changes.
tasks.protocolIcmpDesc: ICMP ping probes run at minute-level intervals to observe latency, minimum, maximum, jitter, and packet loss.
tasks.fieldPortDescTcp: Target port for TCP connect probing, default 443.
tasks.fieldThreadModeDesc: Single-thread mode is for baseline testing, while 8 threads simulate multi-connection throughput.
tasks.fieldIperf3DurationDesc: Seconds for each upload and download action. The backend five-minute claim window covers both actions.
tasks.fieldIperf3ExecutionTimeDesc: Run iperf3 tasks daily in UTC time, formatted as HH:MM. Agent local execution time shifts by its timezone.
tasks.fieldScheduleIntervalDesc: {{protocol}} tasks are scheduled in seconds, minimum 60 seconds. Keep it aligned with chart granularity.
tasks.fieldTimeoutTcpDesc: Milliseconds allowed for one TCP connect attempt.
tasks.fieldTimeoutIcmpDesc: Milliseconds allowed for one ICMP echo request.
tasks.fieldTimeoutMtrDesc: Milliseconds covering the full path probe.
tasks.fieldSampleCountDesc: Number of TCP connect samples per schedule, used to calculate average, min, max, and jitter.
tasks.fieldPacketCountIcmpDesc: ICMP packets sent per schedule, used to calculate average, min, max, and packet loss.
tasks.fieldPayloadSizeTcpDesc: Stored and validated by the backend. Current TCP connect probing does not send payload.
tasks.fieldPayloadSizeIcmpDesc: Maps to ping -s. Backend allows 1 to 65507 bytes, default 64.
tasks.fieldMtrRetryDesc: Trigger retries by cooldown when packet loss exceeds the threshold.
tasks.fieldTtlDesc: Optional. Maps to ping -t when set; empty means no TTL limit.
tasks.fieldDontFragmentDesc: Backend stores dont_fragment. The current Agent ping command does not use this field yet.

targets.managementDesc: Maintain monitoring targets through /api/v1/targets/* and quickly associate Agents.
targets.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, address, or carrier
targets.dialogDesc: Target is the destination address bound to monitoring tasks. Changes sync affected Agent task snapshots.
targets.quickAssociateDesc: Call /api/v1/relations/quick-associate so the backend creates the required monitoring tasks between this Target and Agent.
targets.quickAssociateSuccess: Quick association completed, created or reused {{count}} tasks

results.title: Result Ingestion Events
results.description: View NATS worker ingestion records, duplicate messages, and failure reasons through /api/v1/results/*.

system.notFoundDescription: This address may have changed, or the page does not exist. You can go back or jump to a core page to continue.

alerts.title: Alert Rules
alerts.dialogDesc: Set up a new alert rule for a monitoring task.
alerts.noTasksAvailable: No tasks available. Please create a task first.
alerts.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this alert rule? This action cannot be undone.
alerts.noWebhooksAvailable: No webhooks available. Please create one in the Webhooks page.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.dialogDesc: Add a new webhook endpoint for alert notifications.
webhooks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
webhooks.secretDesc: Save this secret now. It will not be shown again.
webhooks.rotateConfirm: Are you sure you want to rotate the secret for {{name}}? The old secret will be invalidated immediately.
webhooks.editDialogDesc: Modify webhook settings and advanced options.
webhooks.bodyTemplateHint: JSON body with ${variable} placeholders. Leave empty for default payload.
webhooks.customHeadersHint: Additional HTTP headers. Values support ${variable} placeholders.
webhooks.reservedHeaderError: This header is reserved and cannot be overridden.
webhooks.reservedHeadersNote: The following headers are reserved and cannot be set: Content-Type, X-NetPulse-Event, X-NetPulse-Signature, X-NetPulse-Delivery
webhooks.templateVariablesHint: Click to insert at cursor. Use ${variable} syntax in body template and header values.
webhooks.varTriggeredValue: Actual value that triggered the alert

users.title: Users
users.disableConfirm: Are you sure you want to disable {{name}}? They will no longer be able to log in.
users.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters long.

groups.title: Groups
groups.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This will permanently remove the group and all associated user and task assignments. This action cannot be undone.

errorBoundary.title: Page render failed
errorBoundary.description: The error was caught. Retry rendering or refresh the page to continue.

timeRange.invalidRange: Enter valid start and end times.
timeRange.startBeforeEnd: Start time must be before end time.
timeRange.metricsStepHelp: Step is the range query resolution. It controls how often a point is returned between start and end. The backend step_sec accepts 60 to 86400 seconds. Auto is converted to a concrete value in the frontend. Quick ranges automatically advance to the latest window every 1 minute; absolute ranges do not auto-refresh.
timeRange.eventStepHelp: MTR results are queried by actual probe timestamps and do not use metrics aggregation step. Quick ranges automatically advance to the latest window every 1 minute; absolute ranges do not auto-refresh.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.noDataHint: Data will appear once the agent starts reporting
monitoring.noDataHintMulti: Data will appear once agents start reporting
monitoring.selectTask: Select a task to view monitoring data
monitoring.searchPlaceholder: Search Target, Agent, carrier, or task
monitoring.noMatchedTargetsDesc: Adjust search terms or protocol filters.
monitoring.taskMissing: Task does not exist or failed to load
monitoring.mtrTaskMissing: MTR task does not exist or failed to load
monitoring.mtrNoMetricsTitle: MTR does not use metrics charts
monitoring.mtrNoMetricsDesc: MTR data is stored by result and hop. Open the MTR results page to view packet loss, avg, best, worst, and ASN.
monitoring.vmStepSource: Data comes from VictoriaMetrics. Minimum step_sec is 60 seconds.
monitoring.emptyProtocolDesc: Create tasks and generate data to display it here.
monitoring.noAgentSelectedDesc: Select at least one Agent in the Agent filter above.
monitoring.targetDataLoadFailed: Failed to load Target monitoring data
monitoring.targetDataLoadFailedDesc: Unable to read monitoring tasks and metrics by Target.
monitoring.noMtrResultInRange: No MTR result for the selected Agents and time range.
monitoring.selectMtrResultDesc: Select one execution result from the timeline above to inspect hop-level evidence.
monitoring.noIperf3ResultInRange: No iperf3 result for the selected Agents and time range.
monitoring.selectIperf3ResultDesc: Select one execution result from the timeline above to inspect upload, download, retransmits, and execution parameters.

artifacts.description: Manage Agent executable versions, platform metadata, and Cloudflare R2 download entries.
artifacts.activeVersionDesc: When enabled, this version appears in default download candidates.
artifacts.uploadDesc: Choose an Agent executable and fill version and platform metadata. The backend uploads it to Cloudflare R2.
artifacts.editDesc: Only metadata is changed. The file object in Cloudflare R2 is not modified.
artifacts.activeStatusDesc: When disabled, the record is retained but not shown as an available version.
artifacts.deleteDesc: Delete {{version}} ({{platform}})? This soft deletes the record and tries to delete the R2 object.

geoManagement.title: GEO Management
geoManagement.description: Maintain the searchable continent, country, and city catalog used by Target and Agent forms.
geoManagement.searchPlaceholder: Search continents, countries, cities, or codes
geoManagement.dialogDesc: Maintain GEO tree nodes. Target and Agent forms use updated search data immediately after saving.
